response:
C

The universal systems model (input-process-output-feedback) follows a logical sequence for problem-solving: gather data/feedback first (input), involve stakeholders (more input), implement a solution (process), and then evaluate with more data (feedback).

Option C follows this order correctly: gather parking data → receive input from meetings → build parking lot (the actual solution) → gather more data to evaluate.

The other options either start with solutions before gathering input or put steps in an illogical order.
